originalTitle: Dallas rescuers search for victims after gas explosion destroys building and ignites blaze
summary: Four people taken to hospital but mayor said search continues for other survivors or fatalities
content: Nearly 100 firefighters battled a large fire that erupted on Thursday at an apartment complex in Dallas, where debris from one collapsed building lay heaped on the ground while flames and black smoke billowed into the sky. News video showed dozens of firefighters at the scene. Some had hoses trained on piles of smoking debris while others lifted and moved lumber and other burned wreckage as if searching for people underneath. Two firefighters with hoses towered above the blaze on long ladders, while another sprayed water from the window of an upper floor of a neighboring building. A natural gas explosion initially caused the fire, the Dallas fire rescue assistant chief, James Russ, said at a televised press conference. “The fire is contained, but our members are still working on the scene to do primary searches,” Russ said. “We put a drone in the air to canvass the area to see if we see any victims or anything around.” Four people had been taken to the hospital with injuries, Russ said, though he did not know what condition they were in. It was “unknown how many possible fatalities we have”, he added. A nearby street was lined with firetrucks, ambulances and police vehicles with their lights flashing. Julie Jensen said she was at home less than a block from the building when she heard a noise like an explosion that left her ears ringing. “I was sitting on my couch watching TV – stuff flew off our walls,” Jensen said. Jensen said she saw rising smoke and neighbors running when she looked out the window. She grabbed her family’s cat and left, finding a nearby parking lot to wait until she knew it was safe to return. “I am closely monitoring the latest developments regarding the explosion in Oak Cliff,” Dallas’s mayor, Eric Johnson, tweeted. “My prayers are with those injured, their families, and all those who have suffered loss from this tragic event.” A spokesperson for Dallas fire rescue did not immediately return phone and email messages seeking comment. The Dallas police department referred all questions to fire officials.
chineseTitle: 达拉斯救援人员在瓦斯爆炸摧毁建筑物并引发大火后搜寻受害者
chineseSummary: 四人被送往医院，但市长表示仍在继续寻找其他幸存者或死亡者
chineseContent: 周四，达拉斯一处公寓大楼发生大火，近 100 名消防员正在扑灭大火，一栋倒塌建筑的碎片散落在地上，火焰和黑烟滚滚冲上天空。新闻视频显示数十名消防员在现场。一些人将软管对准成堆冒烟的碎片，而另一些人则抬起并移动木材和其他燃烧的残骸，就像在寻找下面的人一样。两名消防员拿着水龙带，站在长梯上，站在火上，另一名消防员则从邻近建筑物上层的窗户喷水。达拉斯消防救援助理队长詹姆斯·拉斯在电视新闻发布会上表示，火灾最初是由天然气爆炸引起的。拉斯说：“火势已得到控制，但我们的成员仍在现场进行初步搜索。” “我们在空中放了一架无人机来搜寻该地区，看看是否看到任何受害者或周围有任何东西。”拉斯说，有四人受伤被送往医院，但他不知道他们的情况如何。他补充说，“目前尚不清楚有多少人可能死亡”。附近的街道两旁停满了消防车、救护车和警车，车灯闪烁。朱莉·詹森 (Julie Jensen) 说，当她听到类似爆炸的声音时，她正在家中，距离大楼不到一个街区，耳朵里嗡嗡作响。 “我坐在沙发上看电视，东西从墙上飞走了，”詹森说。詹森说，当她向窗外望去时，她看到烟雾升起，邻居们在奔跑。她抓起家里的猫离开了，找到附近的一个停车场等待，直到她知道可以安全返回。 “我正在密切关注橡树崖爆炸的最新进展，”达拉斯市长埃里克·约翰逊在推特上写道。 “我为那些受伤的人祈祷
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
